task_completion.visual_evidence: The Sugar Calculate activity interface is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Compute Earth's orbital and escape velocities
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Calculate activity history shows calculations resulting in Earth's orbital velocity (~29.78) and escape velocity (~11.19).

task_completion.description: Compute Mars's orbital and escape velocities
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Calculate activity history shows calculations resulting in Mars's orbital velocity (~24.12 or 24.13) and escape velocity (~5.03).

task_completion.description: Save the session to the Sugar Journal with the specified title
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Sugar Journal interface shows an entry titled 'Orbital Mechanics Problem Set' associated with the Calculate activity.

task_completion.description: Create the results file with the calculated values and labels
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal shows the creation or content of '/home/ga/Documents/orbital_results.txt' containing the four required labels (Earth orbital velocity, Earth escape velocity, Mars orbital velocity, Mars escape velocity) and their corresponding values in km/s.

integrity.description: The agent actually used the Sugar Calculate activity to perform the math.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the formulas being entered and evaluated inside the Calculate activity interface.

integrity.description: The agent did not use terminal-based calculators or scripts to bypass the GUI requirement.
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal windows running Python, bc, or other CLI math tools to compute the velocities.

integrity.description: The agent did not simply type the final answers into the text file without calculating them.
integrity.visual_evidence: The intermediate calculation steps are visible in the Calculate activity before the text file is populated.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Expected values (in km/s, rounded to 2 decimal places): Earth orbital velocity: 29.78, Earth escape velocity: 11.19, Mars orbital velocity: 24.13 (or 24.12), Mars escape velocity: 5.03. The task requires using the Sugar Calculate activity (bundle ID: org.laptop.Calculate), saving the session in the Sugar Journal as 'Orbital Mechanics Problem Set', and writing the results to /home/ga/Documents/orbital_results.txt.
